content: These Terms of Use apply to all matters related to the use of the "Stablecoin Payment Service" (hereinafter referred to as the "SC Payment Service") provided by NetStars Co., Ltd. under the name "SC Payment". When using the SC Payment Service, it is necessary to fully understand the contents of the [Stablecoin Payment Service Terms of Use] (hereinafter referred to as these "Terms of Use") including the appendix, and to acknowledge these Terms of Use.
Article 1 (Merchant)

1 A person (hereinafter referred to as "Party A") who agrees to these Terms of Use and applies to use the SC Payment Service for the purpose of providing the SC Payment Service to their own customers may provide the SC Payment Service as a merchant of NetStars Co., Ltd. (hereinafter referred to as "Party B") when Party B, after conducting its prescribed review, notifies Party A of its approval. At this point, a contract between Party A and Party B regarding Party A providing the SC Payment Service as a merchant of Party B (hereinafter referred to as the "Merchant Contract") shall be established.
2 A person who is not a domestic corporation, the state, or a local public entity shall not be approved in the review set forth in the preceding paragraph.
3 Party A shall, based on these Terms of Use, as a merchant of Party B, obtain consent regarding these Terms of Use from Party A's customers and provide the SC Payment Service to Party A's customers.

4 Party A shall designate stores or facilities that handle the SC Payment Service, notify Party B in writing in advance and obtain Party B's approval (stores or facilities that have obtained such approval are referred to as "Handling Stores"), and shall not handle the SC Payment Service at stores that have not been approved by Party B.

5 Party A shall display merchant signs prescribed by Party B in visible places at terminals and Handling Stores. Party A shall not use the name of the SC Payment Service and its Chinese and English names for purposes other than the execution of business prescribed in these Terms of Use.

6 Party A shall give comprehensive prior consent for Party B to, without obtaining individual consent from Party A, describe the names, locations, etc. of Party A and Party A's Handling Stores as stores where the SC Payment Service is available in printed materials, homepages, and other advertising media, for the purpose of promoting the use of the SC Payment Service by Party A's customers within Japan.

7 Party A shall not use sales totals and other data provided by Party B, terminals lent by Party B, trademarks prescribed in advance by Party B, etc. for purposes other than those prescribed in these Terms of Use, and shall not allow third parties to use them.

8 When Party A receives installation of a payment application on a terminal owned by Party A from Party B, Party A shall pay Party B the application setup fee prescribed in the Terminal/Application Registration Notice without delay after the establishment of the Merchant Contract, and shall pay the application usage fee (annual usage fee) at the time prescribed in such notice. However, it shall be free for one year from the date of execution of this contract. The copyright of the payment application belongs to Party B. Party A shall not use the payment application for purposes other than the execution of obligations prescribed in these Terms of Use.
Article 2 (Formation of Electronic Payment Means Payment Contract / Assignment of Claims)

1 When Party A's customer wishes to make payment through the SC Payment Service, such customer shall apply for a contract (hereinafter referred to as the "Electronic Payment Means Payment Contract") to pay debts borne by Party A's customer regarding the sale of goods or provision of services by Party A (hereinafter referred to as "Sales Transactions, etc.") (hereinafter referred to as "Sales Transaction Debts, etc.", and claims related to Sales Transaction Debts, etc. are hereinafter referred to as "Sales Transaction Claims, etc.") by transferring electronic payment means.
2 Among Party A's customers, the following persons shall not use the SC Payment Service.
(1) Persons who have previously violated these Terms of Use or have been terminated
(2) Minors, adult wards, persons under curatorship, or persons under assistance who have not obtained prior consent from their legal representatives
(3) Persons who are not located in Japan
(4) Heirs of such customer or other persons who have succeeded to the status of such customer

(5) Persons whom Party B determines have a high necessity to suspend their use of the SC Payment Service from the perspective of compliance with domestic and foreign laws, regulations, and guidelines published by regulatory authorities, countermeasures against money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing, and ensuring the effectiveness of economic sanctions
(6) Persons who attempt to make payment through the SC Payment Service using a wallet that Party B does not permit to be used
(7) Persons whom Party B otherwise deems inappropriate
3 Party A's customer shall comply when Party A or Party B requests Party A's customer to provide materials necessary for the review of the application set forth in paragraph 1.

4 The Electronic Payment Means Payment Contract shall be formed when the SC Payment Service QR Code (defined in the next article) is read by a terminal (provided, however, that when the terminal requests input of Party A's customer's PIN, when the PIN is input into the terminal), subject to the condition subsequent that the failure notice (defined in the next article) is given to Party A.

5 At the time of formation of the Electronic Payment Means Payment Contract set forth in the preceding paragraph, Party A shall sell Sales Transaction Claims, etc. to Party B by way of assignment of specified claims for money as consideration, subject to the condition subsequent that the failure notice is given to Party A, and Party A's customer shall consent to this.
Article 3 (Method of Payment through the SC Payment Service)
1 Party B shall, through Party A, provide Party A's customer with the quantity of electronic payment means equivalent to Sales Transaction Debts, etc. (hereinafter referred to as the "Payment Rate").
2 When Party A's customer makes the application set forth in paragraph 1 of the preceding article, such customer shall notify Party B of the following matters by the method prescribed by Party B.
(1) Type and quantity of electronic payment means subject to payment through the SC Payment Service
(2) Type of blockchain ledger on which such electronic payment means are recorded
(3) Wallet address from which such electronic payment means are transferred
(4) Consent to the Payment Rate
(5) Type of wallet used by Party A's customer to transfer such electronic payment means

3 Party A's customer shall sign transaction data for transferring Party A's customer's electronic payment means from the wallet address set forth in item (3) of the preceding paragraph to a wallet address designated by Party B, using the private key necessary to transfer such electronic payment means, by the method prescribed by Party B, and shall notify Party B of the signature result.

4 When Party B determines that Party A's customer has not violated these Terms of Use, Party B shall notify Party A's customer of a QR code (hereinafter referred to as the "SC Payment Service QR Code") linked to the signature result notified by Party A's customer.

5 When Party A's customer applies for an Electronic Payment Means Payment Contract, Party A shall have Party A's customer read the SC Payment Service QR Code displayed on a smartphone, etc. held by Party A's customer with a terminal, or receive a smartphone from Party A's customer and read the SC Payment Service QR Code with a terminal by itself.

6 Party A shall have Party A's customer confirm the amount of Sales Transaction Debts, etc. displayed on the terminal, and then perform the reading set forth in the preceding paragraph [and when the terminal requests input of Party A's customer's PIN, have Party A's customer input the PIN]. [Party A shall have Party A's customer input the PIN by Party A's customer himself/herself, and shall alert Party A's customer to be careful so that the PIN input is not peeked at from behind.]

7 When Party B receives information regarding the SC Payment Service QR Code through Party A, if Party B determines that Party A's customer has not violated these Terms of Use, Party B shall consume the property value (hereinafter referred to as "Gas Fees") necessary to consume to record the transaction data related to such signature result on such blockchain ledger, and then attempt to have such transaction data related to such signature result recorded on such blockchain ledger. Gas Fees shall be borne by Party B.

8 Party A, Party A's customer, and Party B shall treat Sales Transaction Debts, etc. as having been paid when the transaction data related to such signature result is recorded on such blockchain ledger and the transfer of electronic payment means for payment through the SC Payment Service is completed, and thereafter shall not make any claim against Party A's customer regarding Sales Transaction Debts, etc.

9 Electronic payment means received by Party B from Party A's customer shall belong solely to Party B and not to Party A, Party A's customer, or any other third party, at the time when Party A's customer completes the transfer of electronic payment means to a wallet address designated by Party B.

10 Party B shall manage electronic payment means received from Party A's customer for its own benefit and shall not manage them for the benefit of Party A, Party A's customer, or any other third party.

11 When the transfer of electronic payment means is not completed despite the passage of a reasonably necessary period, Party B shall give Party A a notice (hereinafter referred to as the "Failure Notice") that such payment through the SC Payment Service has failed.

12 When the terminal cannot be used for any reason, Party A shall not be able to make payment through the SC Payment Service. In such case, Party B shall not bear any responsibility to Party A for any reason.

13 When Party A recognizes suspicious circumstances that suggest Party A's customer is not using his/her own wallet, Party A shall report to Party B. In addition, when a person other than Party A's customer has improperly used Party A's customer's wallet, if Party A is found to have acted intentionally or with gross negligence, Party A acknowledges that payment may not be made for Sales Transaction Debts, etc. related to such use.

14 Party A shall properly preserve sales slips for goods or services sold to Party A's customer, delivery slips for goods, etc. signed by Party A's customer, and other evidence for at least five years from the transaction date, and shall provide such evidence to Party B based on Party B's request when a dispute arises with Party A's customer in the future.
Article 4 (Handling Amount)
1 The maximum limit per transaction for Sales Transaction Debts, etc. in the SC Payment Service shall be 2,000,000 yen.

2 If Party A wishes to set a maximum or minimum limit per transaction for Sales Transaction Debts, etc. in addition to the maximum limit set forth in the preceding paragraph, Party A must consult with Party B in advance and obtain Party B's consent.
Article 5 (Prohibition of Refusal of Payment and Discriminatory Treatment through the SC Payment Service)

1 Party A shall not refuse payment through the SC Payment Service without justifiable reason, require the use of cash payment or credit cards or other payment means, or charge different prices from cash-paying customers under any name such as handling fees, etc., or otherwise treat Party A's customers who present valid SC Payment Service QR Codes disadvantageously.
2 When requested by Party B, Party A shall promptly cooperate in investigations regarding Party A's customers' use of the SC Payment Service, etc.
Article 6 (Cash and Other Prohibited Goods)
1 Party A shall not conduct fictitious transactions, and shall not handle goods or services that fall under or are likely to fall under any of the following.
(1) Those determined to be contrary to public order and morals
(2) Those that violate the Swords and Firearms Control Law, the Narcotics Control Law, the Washington Convention, and other related laws and regulations
(3) Those that infringe third parties' copyrights, portrait rights, trademark rights, and other intellectual property rights and other rights
(4) Cash, gift certificates, stamps, postage stamps, commuter passes, prepaid cards, securities, crypto-assets, electronic payment means, and other highly liquid goods equivalent to these

2 When Party B requests a report on goods or services handled, Party A shall promptly report, and if Party B determines that they fall under any of the items of the preceding paragraph, Party A shall immediately suspend the sale of such goods or services.
Article 7 (Cancellation of Payment)

1 When an Electronic Payment Means Payment Contract is lawfully dissolved by cancellation (including cancellation by agreement) or rescission, etc. (including dissolution of an Electronic Payment Means Payment Contract due to dissolution of Sales Transactions, etc.), Party A shall notify Party B that it is necessary to return the electronic payment means received by Party B to Party A's customer, and in no case shall Party A directly refund or return to Party A's customer.

2 When Party B receives the notice set forth in the preceding paragraph or deems it necessary, Party B shall return electronic payment means by directly transferring electronic payment means to Party A's customer by the method prescribed by Party B.
3 When the return of electronic payment means is completed, Party B shall notify Party A to that effect.

4 At the time when the notice set forth in the preceding paragraph is completed, Party A shall bear a refund obligation to Party B, but this shall be settled by offsetting against Party B's payment to Party A set forth in paragraph 1 of Article 8.
Article 8 (Settlement of Sale of Sales Transaction Claims, etc.)

1 Payment by Party B to Party A of the consideration for the sale of Sales Transaction Claims, etc. conducted based on paragraph 5 of Article 2 during the period from the first day to the last day of each calendar month shall be made by bank transfer on the last day of the month following such month, of the amount obtained by deducting the handling fee set forth in the next article from the face amount of Sales Transaction Claims, etc. However, if such day is not a bank business day, it shall be the next bank business day.

2 When payment set forth in paragraph 1 cannot be made by the payment due date due to changes in domestic and foreign laws, strengthening of domestic and foreign remittance regulations, strengthening of regulations on transfer of electronic payment means in Japan and abroad, system down by hackers, or other force majeure, Party B shall not bear responsibility to Party A for damages due to such delay. However, Party A and Party B shall consult in good faith regarding measures for such cases.
3 Party B shall not transfer electronic payment means to Party A in the SC Payment Service.
Article 9 (Handling Fees)
Party A shall pay Party B a handling fee calculated at the rate prescribed in the merchant application form for the amount of Sales Transaction Claims, etc.
Article 10 (Disputes with Party A's Customers, etc.)

1 Party A shall bear all responsibility regarding goods, services, etc. for which payment was made through the SC Payment Service, and when receiving complaints or consultations from Party A's customers or when disputes arise with Party A's customers, Party A shall promptly resolve them at its own responsibility.

2 When Party B determines that an investigation is necessary regarding Party A or transactions, etc. by Party A based on these Terms of Use, or complaints from Party A's customers, or other necessary matters, Party B may conduct or request an investigation of Party A, and Party A must promptly cooperate in such investigation.
Article 11 (Prohibition of Assignment)
1 Party A and Party A's customers shall not assign their legal status based on these Terms of Use to third parties.
2 Party A and Party A's customers shall not assign or pledge their claims against Party B to third parties.
Article 12 (Confidentiality)

1 Party A and Party B shall not provide, disclose, or leak to third parties, or use for purposes other than the performance of these Terms of Use, technical or business secrets or other secrets (hereinafter referred to as "Trade Secrets, etc.") of the other party (the other party for Party A is Party B, and the other party for Party B is Party A. The same applies hereinafter in this article) learned in the course of performing these Terms of Use, without the prior written consent of the other party.
2 Trade Secrets, etc. set forth in the preceding paragraph shall include information on business communications, etc. provided by Party B to Party A.
3 Party A and Party B shall take necessary measures to prevent loss, damage, leakage, etc. of Trade Secrets, etc., and shall be responsible for loss, damage, leakage, etc. of such information.

4 When Party A entrusts part or all of business processing, etc. related to these Terms of Use to a third party, Party A shall inform such contractor of the obligations set forth in this article and conduct necessary management. In addition, even if such contractor violates matters prescribed in these Terms of Use, Party A shall not be exempted from the responsibilities set forth in these Terms of Use.
5 The provisions of this article shall remain in effect even after the termination of the Merchant Contract.
Article 13 (Handling of Personal Information)

1 Party A shall keep confidential all information concerning Party A's customers (hereinafter referred to as "Personal Information, etc.", regardless of recording or storage media) learned in the course of performing these Terms of Use.

2 When Party A violates the provisions of the preceding paragraph and loss or leakage of Personal Information, etc. occurs or is likely to occur, Party A shall immediately report to Party B and take appropriate measures to prevent secondary damage and expansion of other damage.

3 Party A shall comply with the Act on the Protection of Personal Information and applicable personal information protection guidelines to prevent loss, damage, leakage, etc. of Personal Information, etc., and shall take necessary measures prescribed therein and be responsible for loss, damage, leakage, etc. of Personal Information, etc.

4 Party A shall properly store Personal Information, etc. at its own responsibility, and when the Merchant Contract is terminated, Party A shall immediately dispose of it in accordance with Party B's instructions. However, this shall not apply when Party A is required to store such Personal Information, etc. for a certain period under laws, company rules, etc.

5 When Party A entrusts part or all of business processing, etc. related to these Terms of Use to a third party, Party A shall obtain Party B's prior approval and select a contractor that meets sufficient personal information protection standards. In addition, even if such contractor violates matters prescribed in these Terms of Use, Party A shall not be exempted from the responsibilities set forth in these Terms of Use.
6 The provisions of this article shall remain in effect even after the termination of the Merchant Contract.
Article 14 (Prohibited Matters)
1 Party A and Party A's customers shall not reproduce, adapt, alter, or reverse engineer applications and other programs and systems provided by Party B without authorization.
2 Party A and Party A's customers shall not engage in money laundering or cooperate in such activities.
3 Party A and Party A's customers shall not use the SC Payment Service for the movement of funds related to gambling or related activities.
4 Party A and Party A's customers shall not use the SC Payment Service for the contribution or provision of money or other property.
5 Party A and Party A's customers shall not use the SC Payment Service for purposes that violate legal provisions or public order and morals.

6 Party A and Party A's customers shall not use the SC Payment Service for transactions, payments, etc. or other acts that require permission, approval, notification, or other procedures under the Foreign Exchange and Foreign Trade Act.
7 When making payment through the SC Payment Service, Party A's customer shall not use a wallet that Party B does not permit.
8 When making payment through the SC Payment Service, Party A's customer shall not transfer the following electronic payment means to Party B.
(1) Electronic payment means belonging to third parties
(2) Electronic payment means subject to security, deposit, loan, quasi-joint ownership, or other encumbrances

(3) Electronic payment means that are in a state different from the state at the time of issuance due to events other than changes by the issuer of such electronic payment means, such as splitting, processing, modification, wrapping, bridging, etc.
(4) Electronic payment means used in fraudulent or other criminal acts
(5) Electronic payment means obtained by false or other improper means
9 When making payment through the SC Payment Service, Party A's customer shall not consume Gas Fees by itself to have transaction data related to such payment recorded on the blockchain ledger.
Article 15 (Change of Reported Matters)

1 When changes occur in important matters reported to Party B, such as trade name, representative name, location, Handling Stores, contact information, and designated financial institution accounts, Party A shall promptly report to Party B.

2 If notices or documents sent from the other party are delayed or do not arrive due to Party A's failure to make the report set forth in the preceding paragraph, they shall be deemed to have arrived at the time when they should normally arrive.
3 If the provision of the SC Payment Service is delayed due to Party A's failure to make the report set forth in paragraph 1, Party B shall not bear any responsibility.
Article 16 (Contract Period)

The effective period of the Merchant Contract shall be one year from the date on which Party B approves Party A's merchant application. However, unless either party gives written notice to the other party of its intention not to renew at least three months before the expiration of the effective period, it shall be automatically extended for one year, and the same shall apply thereafter.
Article 17 (Cancellation)
1 Either Party A or Party B may cancel the Merchant Contract by giving notice to the other party in writing with a notice period of three months or more.

2 Notwithstanding the provisions of the preceding paragraph, Party B may terminate the provision of the payment system through the SC Payment Service due to changes in social conditions, revision or abolition of laws, or other circumstances of Party B, and in such case, Party B may immediately cancel the Merchant Contract by giving notice to Party A in writing.
3 Even if damages (including lost profits and opportunity losses) occur to Party A due to the provisions of the preceding paragraph, Party B shall not bear any responsibility.
Article 18 (Termination)
Either Party A or Party B may immediately terminate the Merchant Contract without any demand when the other party falls under any of the following, and may claim damages if damages occur as a result.
(1) When the other party violates these Terms of Use
(2) When the other party receives cancellation or other administrative dispositions of licenses or registrations required for business
(3) When bills or checks drawn or endorsed by the other party are dishonored
(4) When the other party receives compulsory execution, auction applications, preservation dispositions, or delinquency dispositions, etc.
(5) When the other party receives or files applications for bankruptcy, civil rehabilitation, or corporate reorganization
(6) When it is determined that there has been a significant change in credit status, in addition to the preceding three items
(7) When the other party is dissolved other than by merger
(8) When Party A made false applications in applying for the Merchant Contract
(9) When Party B determines that Party A is inappropriate as a merchant of Party B due to Party A's goods, services, or sales methods, etc., complaints from Party A's customers, or other reasons
Article 19 (Post-Termination Processing)
1 When the Merchant Contract is terminated, Party A must immediately remove merchant signs.
2 After the termination of the Merchant Contract, Party B shall not use the other party's trademarks, names, etc. without individual consent.

3 When the Merchant Contract is terminated, Electronic Payment Means Payment Contracts conducted by the termination date of the Merchant Contract shall remain valid, and Party A and Party B shall handle such contracts in accordance with these Terms of Use. However, this shall not apply when Party A and Party B separately agree otherwise.
Article 20 (Intellectual Property Rights)

1 All rights regarding all resources constituting the SC Payment Service shall belong to Party B or third parties having such rights, except as prescribed in these Terms of Use, and Party A and Party A's customers may only use the SC Payment Service.

2 Party A and Party A's customers shall not, without Party B's permission, engage in any acts that infringe all intellectual property rights or ownership rights of Party B or third parties, including copyrights, trademarks, portrait rights, publicity rights, and rights related to content materials.
Article 21 (Disclaimer)

1 The SC Payment Service may be changed, functions may be added, or the provision of such functions may be suspended without prior notice, etc., and new restrictions may be imposed on the use of the SC Payment Service. In addition, it may not be possible to provide all or part of the SC Payment Service due to defects in communication networks, malfunctions, failures, use not based on prescribed usage methods, or other obstacles.

2 When Party A's customers suffer damages due to Party B's reservation of use of the SC Payment Service, suspension or termination of the SC Payment Service, change or deletion of content, change of these Terms of Use, or other matters arising from the SC Payment Service, in accordance with these Terms of Use, Party B shall not be liable for damages except when Party B has acted intentionally or negligently. In addition, when damages occur to Party A's customers in connection with the SC Payment Service due to Party B's breach of contract or tort, Party B shall be liable only within the scope of damages that normally occur (excluding lost profits and other damages arising from special circumstances) except when Party B has acted intentionally or with gross negligence.
3 Party B does not warrant the truth, accuracy, currency, usefulness, reliability, legality of the contents of the SC Payment Service, or that third-party rights are not infringed.

4 The SC Payment Service is provided as-is, and Party B does not warrant that there are no factual or legal defects (including but not limited to defects, errors, bugs, and infringement of rights related to safety, reliability, accuracy, effectiveness, security, etc.) or that there is no unauthorized access by third parties to Party B's systems, etc.
5 The SC Payment Service is a service provided to Party A's customers located in Japan, and Party B does not warrant that the SC Payment Service is factually or legally available outside Japan.

6 Party A's customers shall manage their own crypto-assets, electronic payment means, tokens, and other digital assets on the blockchain (hereinafter referred to as "Digital Assets") at their own responsibility when using the SC Payment Service. Party B does not manage private keys, passphrases, mnemonics, or other information (hereinafter referred to as "Private Keys, etc.") necessary for Party A's customers to transfer Digital Assets when providing the SC Payment Service, and cannot actively transfer such Digital Assets of Party A's customers.

7 Party B shall not be liable for loss or leakage of users' Digital Assets or Private Keys, etc. due to theft, loss, misdelivery, or other causes, except when Party B has acted intentionally or negligently.

8 Even when Party A's customers need to use cloud services, blockchain nodes, RPC endpoints, wallets, or other external services when using the SC Payment Service, Party A's customers shall recognize that Party B does not operate or maintain these services and shall use such services at their own responsibility. Party B shall not be liable for losses or damages to Party A's customers arising from external services, except when Party B has acted intentionally or negligently.

9 Party B may temporarily suspend the SC Payment Service at its discretion when there are unavoidable circumstances such as sudden changes in the market price of electronic payment means, system abnormalities related to the SC Payment Service, or legal amendments related to the SC Payment Service.
Article 22 (Exclusion of Anti-Social Forces)
1 Party A and Party A's customers shall declare and warrant the following matters.

(1) That they themselves and their officers and employees in important positions or advisors equivalent thereto (hereinafter referred to as "Officers, etc.") are not and have not been anti-social forces (meaning "anti-social forces" as defined in the "Guidelines for Companies to Prevent Damage by Anti-Social Forces" announced by the Ministerial Meeting on Measures against Crime on June 19, 2007. The same applies hereinafter)
(2) That they themselves and their Officers, etc. do not use the power of anti-social forces for their own unjust gains or for any other purpose
(3) That they themselves and their Officers, etc. do not provide funds to anti-social forces or otherwise cooperate in the maintenance or operation of anti-social forces
(4) That they themselves and their Officers, etc. do not have relationships with anti-social forces that should be socially condemned

(5) That they themselves and their Officers, etc. do not engage in violent demand acts against the other party by themselves or through third parties, and do not make unjust demands beyond the scope of legal responsibility, damage the other party's reputation or credit, or interfere with the other party's business
(6) That they do not have persons who fall under anti-social forces perform all or part of business related to these Terms of Use

2 When facts of violation of any of the items of the preceding paragraph are discovered, Party A and Party A's customers shall immediately notify Party B. In addition, when Party B specifically suspects that Party A or Party A's customer has violated any of the items of the preceding paragraph, Party B may request a report on such matters, and Party A and Party A's customers shall report on such matters within a reasonable period when requested.
3 When Party A or Party A's customer violates the provisions of this article, Party B may immediately terminate all or part of the Merchant Contract without any notice or demand.

4 When Party B suffers damages due to Party A's or Party A's customer's violation of the provisions of this article, Party B may claim damages against Party A and Party A's customer, respectively, based on these Terms of Use regarding such damages, regardless of contract termination based on the preceding paragraph. In addition, Party B shall not bear any responsibility to compensate for damages to Party A and Party A's customers arising from termination of the Merchant Contract based on the provisions of the preceding paragraph, and when Party A or Party A's customer terminates, Party B shall be released from all obligations to Party A and Party A's customers at such time.
Article 23 (Revision and Acknowledgment of these Terms of Use)

1 When Party B revises these Terms of Use, Party B shall notify Party A of the new terms or publish them by an appropriate method. When Party A provides the SC Payment Service to Party A's customers after receiving such notice or after publication, Party A shall be deemed to have acknowledged the new terms, and the new terms shall apply to subsequent handling, etc.

2 Party B may change the contents of these Terms by publishing or notifying by posting on Party B's homepage or other methods that Party B deems appropriate when any of the following applies, and the changed contents shall apply after the change date.
(1) When the change to these Terms is in the general interest of Party A and Party A's customers

(2) When the change to these Terms does not violate the purpose of these Terms, and is reasonable in light of the necessity of the change, the appropriateness of the contents after the change, the contents of the change, and other circumstances related to the change
Article 24 (Governing Law)
These Terms of Use shall be governed by and construed in accordance with Japanese law.
Article 25 (Jurisdiction)
All disputes arising in connection with these Terms of Use shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Tokyo District Court.
The above
[Enforcement and Revision]
November [20], Reiwa 7 (2025) - First Edition Enforced
Appendix
Points to Note Regarding the SC Payment Service
The SC Payment Service is provided based on these Terms of Use, and the specific contents are as follows.
1 Regarding the contents of the SC Payment Service
...[Contents of the SC Payment Service, parties, spread, etc...]
2 Regarding electronic payment means that can be used in the SC Payment Service
...[Name, ticker, ledger, contract address, issuer, etc...]
3 Guidelines regarding hard forks

...[When the scope of records on the blockchain that form the basis of such electronic payment means becomes unclear due to blockchain forks or other reasons, Party B may determine the scope of records on the blockchain that form the basis of such electronic payment means in relation to the SC Payment Service, etc...]
4 Regarding risks of the SC Payment Service
[Risks, the following contents, etc...]

(1) Party A and Party A's customers shall recognize that there are risks in payment using electronic payment means through the SC Payment Service and shall bear such risks. Examples of risks include the following, but are not limited to these.
a Digital assets such as electronic payment means may be lost and losses may be incurred due to unintended transactions conducted by themselves or third parties.

b The transfer of electronic payment means may be deemed completed when recorded on a blockchain or other distributed ledger, and transactions may not be evaluated as having been conducted at the intended time.
c When accessing hardware, software, or the Internet in using the SC Payment Service, users may be hacked, and digital assets such as electronic payment means may be lost and losses may be incurred.
d Significant impacts such as damages may occur due to blockchain upgrades, hard forks, specification changes, failures, suspensions, or other reasons.

e Significant restrictions may arise on the acquisition, holding, or use of electronic payment means due to domestic and foreign laws, regulations, guidelines, or judgments or dispositions by authorities applicable to electronic payment means and other digital assets.

f Digital assets such as electronic payment means may be lost and losses may occur due to loss or theft of Private Keys, etc. necessary to transfer electronic payment means and other digital assets, or inability to access cloud services, wallets, or other external services used to manage Private Keys, etc.
g Damages may occur due to the disappearance or decrease in value of electronic payment means or assets backing them when issuers or managers of electronic payment means fail.

h The market price of electronic payment means constantly fluctuates due to prices, currency values, securities market trends, security risks, and other events, and losses may be incurred due to such price fluctuations.

i The liquidity (trading volume) of such electronic payment means may decrease, and users may become unable to effectively utilize electronic payment means they hold, or may be forced to trade at significantly unfavorable prices, due to evaluations of electronic payment means, introduction of legal regulations, natural disasters, wars, political changes, legal changes, industry strikes (strikes, etc.), and other unexpected future events.

(2) Party A and Party A's customers shall fully understand blockchain technology, electronic payment means and other digital assets, mechanisms and other functions of wallets provided by third parties, recognize the risks and impacts of acquiring or holding electronic payment means, and use electronic payment means for payment through the SC Payment Service after complying with matters prescribed in these Terms.
